youtube_creator_observation_ledger.source_policy_posture: Static YouTube creator/channel observation ledger generated from the full 200-row admitted fragrance Shorts pool and reconciled against the external Orca data-lake raw packets. One-platform observation only; cross-platform identity/linkage, metrics rollups, audience, outreach, and SQLite/runtime storage are out of scope.

source_inputs.role: creator grouping, handle-query labels, observed author names, pool membership, and transcript word-count bounds
raw YouTube caption/audio packet metadata reconciliation for every video id

niche_scope.membership_basis: admitted transcript-bearing fragrance Shorts pool; not proof of creator-wide niche exclusivity

metric_rollup_policy.reason: The reconciled lake packets preserve captions/audio metadata, not public view/like/comment metrics. Average views and engagement rate belong in metric observation and rollup records, then join into creator_profile_current.
metric_rollup_policy.open_next: capture YouTube per-video metric observations from RSS/watch surfaces before computing creator rollups

creator_observations.identity_boundary: observed YouTube channel/handle-query evidence only; not verified person identity, ownership, independence, or cross-platform account linkage
limitations: creator_handle_query is a capture/query label, not a guaranteed current YouTube @handle
channel/display labels are mutable public observations
current caption/audio packets do not carry views, likes, comments, average views, or engagement rate
cross-platform TikTok/Instagram linkage is not claimed by this row

accepted_residuals: Creator handle-query values are source/capture labels and may not be current YouTube @handles.
One JeremyFragrance video packet lacks a channel_id in its capture_metadata.json; the creator-level channel key is still resolved from the remaining matched packets and source ledger.
The current lake has 200 YouTube raw packets for this pool, but only one Instagram creator audio packet and no TikTok creator/account-link evidence for cross-platform stitching.
Caption/audio packets do not contain view, like, comment, average-view, or engagement-rate metrics.
